title: I One-Shotted An Entire App With Claude Fable 5 (Full Breakdown)
summary: Jordan Urbs demonstrates building a video editing app in one hour by feeding Claude Fable 5 only screenshots of a SaaS landing page via Claude Code. The model successfully generated functional code including filler removal, clip detection, and subtitle export, though Fable 5 consumes API limits 2x faster than Opus and will be discontinued on June 22.
writeup: A first-person trial run of Claude 4.5 inside Claude Code, building a local video editor MVP from Descript landing-page screenshots and a Venice API key in under an hour. The video centers on a concrete billing warning: Claude 4.5 ships in all Claude subscription plans only through a promo period ending June 22nd, after which it requires direct API billing and burns usage limits twice as fast as Opus. The through-line is an honest, unsponsored assessment of whether the capability jump justifies the cost step-up for builders already invested in harness-based, IDE-native workflows.
key_points: Claude 4.5 is accessible by running 'claude update' in Claude Code, but the promo inclusion in Pro and Max subscription plans ends June 22nd, after which it costs twice as much per usage limit unit as Opus and bills against the API directly rather than the subscription.

The one-shot app build from Descript landing-page screenshots produced a working local video editor (named Redline) within a single session: filler-word removal and clip export ran on short video files, though longer MP4s failed to parse, showing the initial build was not fully production-complete.

The AI editing features inside the built app ran on Llama 3.3 via OpenRouter rather than a top-tier model, which the creator identified as a wiring limitation of the generated app rather than a failure of Claude 4.5 itself.

The creator's existing content-repurposing workflow runs entirely inside VS Codium via a CLAUDE.md-configured multi-agent harness with specialized subagents (blog writer, content ideator, newsletter writer, video script writer, voice reviewer) and a brand knowledge base, processing SRT transcripts with no UI required.

Claude 4.5 was described as capable enough that a product demo could plausibly be built live during a sales call, representing a qualitative shift from earlier agentic models that required detailed spec-writing and constant oversight.

Uncertainty remains about whether Claude 4.5 is decisively better than Opus 4.8 for app building, since the creator had strong Opus results and had not directly compared the two on recent coding tasks.
